<!--
Thanks for opening a pull request. The checklist below mirrors the
conventions in CONTRIBUTING.md. Delete sections that do not apply.
-->

## Summary

<!-- One or two sentences. What does this PR change, and why is it needed? Avoid restating the diff. -->

## Related issue

<!-- "Closes #123" or "Refs #45". Leave blank if there is no linked issue. -->

## Type of change

- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that changes existing behaviour)
- [ ] Documentation only
- [ ] Refactor or internal cleanup (no user-visible change)

## Checklist

- [ ] One concern per PR. Bug fixes and refactors are not bundled.
- [ ] Tests added or updated. New features land with a test in `tests/`. Bug fixes land with a regression test that fails before the patch and passes after.
- [ ] `pytest -q` passes locally.
- [ ] No unrelated formatting churn.
- [ ] CHANGELOG entry added under the next-version block, if user-visible.
- [ ] Documentation updated (README, CONTRIBUTING, or in-CLI help) if behaviour or surface changed.

## Screenshots or output (for UI or output-format changes)

<!-- Paste a screenshot, a screen recording, or the relevant before-and-after CLI output here. -->

## Notes for reviewers

<!-- Anything specific you want a reviewer to look at? Edge cases you weren't sure about? Performance trade-offs? -->
